items.title: In speech to nation, Trump declassifies intelligence claiming Chinese interference in 2020 elections
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump declassified intelligence he said showed Chinese interference in US elections, reviving claims about election security despite intelligence findings that Beijing did not alter the 2020 vote he lost. The move reinforces Trump's push to make election integrity a central issue ahead of November's congressional midterm elections.
items.content: US President Donald Trump declassified intelligence he said showed Chinese interference in US elections, reviving claims about election security despite intelligence findings that Beijing did not alter the 2020 vote he lost. The move reinforces Trump's push to make election integrity a central issue ahead of November's congressional midterm elections.

items.title: Former boss of Italian motorways sentenced to 12 years over Genoa bridge tragedy
items.standfirst: Giovanni Castellucci among 32 people convicted over the 2018 Morandi Bridge collapse, which killed 43Thirty-two people, including the former chief executive of Italy’s motorway operator, have been convicted over the 2018 collapse of a Genoa bridge in which 43 people died.In a hushed courtroom on Thursday in the north-western Italian city, Giovanni Castellucci, a former boss of Autostrade per l’Italia, was sentenced to 12 years in prison, the highest in the case. Continue reading...
items.content: Giovanni Castellucci among 32 people convicted over the 2018 Morandi Bridge collapse, which killed 43Thirty-two people, including the former chief executive of Italy’s motorway operator, have been convicted over the 2018 collapse of a Genoa bridge in which 43 people died.In a hushed courtroom on Thursday in the north-western Italian city, Giovanni Castellucci, a former boss of Autostrade per l’Italia, was sentenced to 12 years in prison, the highest in the case. Continue reading...

items.title: Genoa bridge collapse trial highlights Italy's ageing infrastructure issue
items.standfirst: An Italian court sentenced 32 defendants for their role in a deadly bridge disaster eight years ago that killed 43 people in Genoa. One of the accused was Autostrade's former chief executive, Giovanni Castellucci, was found guilty of vehicular homicide and negligence and was sentenced to twelve years in prison. Besides Castellucci, the former head of Spea, the engineering company in charge of the renovations, as well as officials from the infrastructure ministry were on trial as well. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta tells us more on the trial.
items.content: An Italian court sentenced 32 defendants for their role in a deadly bridge disaster eight years ago that killed 43 people in Genoa. One of the accused was Autostrade's former chief executive, Giovanni Castellucci, was found guilty of vehicular homicide and negligence and was sentenced to twelve years in prison. Besides Castellucci, the former head of Spea, the engineering company in charge of the renovations, as well as officials from the infrastructure ministry were on trial as well. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta tells us more on the trial.

items.title: Former Italian motorway boss sentenced over Genoa bridge tragedy
items.standfirst: The former head of Italy's motorway operator has been sentenced to 12 years in prison for his role in the Genoa bridge collapse that killed 43 people.
items.content: The former head of Italy's motorway operator has been sentenced to 12 years in prison for his role in the Genoa bridge collapse that killed 43 people.

clusters.title: How testosterone testing will work in Hegseth's 'manly' military
clusters.neutral_headline: How testosterone testing will work in Hegseth's 'manly' military
bullet_summary: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has announced mandatory testosterone screening for military personnel aged 30 and over
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday that military personnel age 30 and older will undergo testing for testosterone deficiency as part of annual health screenings
"It's well-established science that as we age, testosterone levels often naturally drop," Hegseth said in a video posted on X along with the text: "The High-T Department of War
Doctors raised a range of concerns about a testosterone screening policy announced by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, which is likely to be costly to implement

items.title: How testosterone testing will work in Hegseth's 'manly' military
items.standfirst: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has announced mandatory testosterone screening for military personnel aged 30 and over.
items.content: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has announced mandatory testosterone screening for military personnel aged 30 and over.

items.title: Pete Hegseth’s Plan for ‘High T’ Troops Is a Junk-Science Fever Dream
items.standfirst: The defense secretary’s idea of administering testosterone therapy to members of the US Armed Forces reveals little understanding of the complexity of hormones.
items.content: The defense secretary’s idea of administering testosterone therapy to members of the US Armed Forces reveals little understanding of the complexity of hormones.

items.title: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announces testosterone screening for US troops
items.standfirst: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday that military personnel age 30 and older will undergo testing for testosterone deficiency as part of annual health screenings. "It's well-established science that as we age, testosterone levels often naturally drop," Hegseth said in a video posted on X along with the text: "The High-T Department of War." FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
items.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday that military personnel age 30 and older will undergo testing for testosterone deficiency as part of annual health screenings. "It's well-established science that as we age, testosterone levels often naturally drop," Hegseth said in a video posted on X along with the text: "The High-T Department of War." FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

items.title: Doctors question plan to screen military members for low testosterone
items.standfirst: Doctors raised a range of concerns about a testosterone screening policy announced by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, which is likely to be costly to implement.
items.content: Doctors raised a range of concerns about a testosterone screening policy announced by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, which is likely to be costly to implement.

items.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
items.standfirst: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.
items.content: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

items.title: Doctor on how concerning wildfire smoke is for health
items.standfirst: Millions of Americans across the Northeast and Midwest were under air quality alerts this week as smoke from Canadian and Minnesota wildfires settled in. At the same time, some of those same areas saw severe heat. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.
items.content: Millions of Americans across the Northeast and Midwest were under air quality alerts this week as smoke from Canadian and Minnesota wildfires settled in. At the same time, some of those same areas saw severe heat. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

items.standfirst: Smoke from more than 800 blazes has filled major cities from Toronto and New York, to parts of the US Midwest and Great Lakes.
items.content: Smoke from more than 800 blazes has filled major cities from Toronto and New York, to parts of the US Midwest and Great Lakes.

items.title: Watch: Wildfire smoke blankets cities across U.S., Canada
items.standfirst: Heavy smoke from wildfires in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing parts of the U.S., prompting air quality alerts in 18 states. These videos show the hazy conditions on the ground in several cities.
items.content: Heavy smoke from wildfires in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing parts of the U.S., prompting air quality alerts in 18 states. These videos show the hazy conditions on the ground in several cities.

items.title: Air quality plummets in 20 US states as smoke from Canadian wildfires spreads
items.standfirst: Millions of Americans face air quality alerts from Minnesota to New York as authorities urge people to stay indoorsSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailTell us: have you been affected by the spread of wildfire smoke in the US and Canada?Smoke from wildfires burning in south-central Canada and parts of Minnesota is spreading across the US, prompting air quality alerts in more than 20 states with millions of Americans expected to face unhealthy air conditions this week.The smoke from the more than 180 active wildfires in northern Ontario made Chicago’s air quality the worst in the world on Thursday evening, followed by Detroit and Minneapolis, according to IQAir’s global rankings. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Dangerous wildfire smoke continues to blanket parts of the U.S.
items.standfirst: Orange skies and heavy smoke covered large parts of the U.S. on Thursday as Canada and Minnesota grapple with wildfires. Hazardous air quality is expected to continue.
items.content: Orange skies and heavy smoke covered large parts of the U.S. on Thursday as Canada and Minnesota grapple with wildfires. Hazardous air quality is expected to continue.(Image credit: Spencer Platt)

items.title: Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world
items.standfirst: Environment Canada has issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellowSmoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the current worst in the world and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the north-east US.Environment Canada issued health warnings on Wednesday after the sky over country’s largest city turned a sickly yellow and was ranked the worst in the world according to IQAir, the Swiss technology company that racks global air quality. Continue reading...
items.content: Environment Canada has issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellowSmoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the current worst in the world and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the north-east US.Environment Canada issued health warnings on Wednesday after the sky over country’s largest city turned a sickly yellow and was ranked the worst in the world according to IQAir, the Swiss technology company that racks global air quality. clusters.title: Zelensky's dismissal of Ukraine's defence chief sparks political crisis
clusters.neutral_headline: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World, BBC World and others

items.title: Zelensky's dismissal of Ukraine's defence chief sparks political crisis
items.standfirst: Rare protests erupted in Ukraine on Thursday over the dismissal of defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov as a dispute between the reformer and Kyiv's top general spilled into the open during the president's second overhaul of his wartime cabinet in a year. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.
items.content: Rare protests erupted in Ukraine on Thursday over the dismissal of defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov as a dispute between the reformer and Kyiv's top general spilled into the open during the president's second overhaul of his wartime cabinet in a year. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Bitter row splits Ukraine's military leadership after defence minister ousted
items.standfirst: Ukraine's ousted defence minister directed stunning public criticism at the army's top commander on Thursday, forcing President Volodymyr Zelensky to call for unity amid signs of an emerging split in the military's top ranks. Large protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities against the removal of popular defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who had been brought in six months ago to digitise and modernise an army fatigued after four years fighting off the Russian invasion. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg reports.
items.content: Ukraine's ousted defence minister directed stunning public criticism at the army's top commander on Thursday, forcing President Volodymyr Zelensky to call for unity amid signs of an emerging split in the military's top ranks. Large protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities against the removal of popular defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who had been brought in six months ago to digitise and modernise an army fatigued after four years fighting off the Russian invasion. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg reports.

items.title: Political crisis and protests in Ukraine as Zelenskyy defends sacking defence minister
items.standfirst: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leadersUkraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership.Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challenging dialogue” between Fedorov – widely seen as a reformist and moderniser – and the military’s commander in chief, Col Gen Oleksandr Syrskyi. Continue reading...
items.content: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leadersUkraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership.Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challenging dialogue” between Fedorov – widely seen as a reformist and moderniser – and the military’s commander in chief, Col Gen Oleksandr Syrskyi. Continue reading...

items.title: How rivalry with a top general brought down Ukraine’s popular defence minister Fedorov
items.standfirst: The forced resignation of Ukraine's 35-year-old defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov in a sweeping government reshuffle on Wednesday has exposed competing visions within the country’s civil and military leadership on how best to hold back the Russian advance.
items.content: The forced resignation of Ukraine's 35-year-old defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov in a sweeping government reshuffle on Wednesday has exposed competing visions within the country’s civil and military leadership on how best to hold back the Russian advance.

items.title: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
items.standfirst: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.
items.content: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.

items.title: Ukraine: Protests erupt after defence minister sacked
items.standfirst: With demonstrators shouting “Shame!” and “Power to the people!”, rare protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities on Thursday over the dismissal of the country’s popular defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Seen as a moderniser, Fedorov was removed by President Volodymyr Zelensky after only six months in the job in what many see as a result of infighting within the Ukrainian military.
items.content: With demonstrators shouting “Shame!” and “Power to the people!”, rare protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities on Thursday over the dismissal of the country’s popular defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Seen as a moderniser, Fedorov was removed by President Volodymyr Zelensky after only six months in the job in what many see as a result of infighting within the Ukrainian military.

items.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's tech-savvy defense minister in government reshuffle
items.standfirst: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired the country's popular defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, who pushed for innovation on the battlefield through use of drones and turned the tables on Russia.
items.content: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired the country's popular defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, who pushed for innovation on the battlefield through use of drones and turned the tables on Russia.(Image credit: ROMAN PILIPEY)

clusters.title: Likweli: A new monkey species discovered in the Congo Basin
clusters.neutral_headline: Watch: What we know about newly discovered monkey species
bullet_summary: Reported by Reuters Top News, BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Likweli: A new monkey species discovered in the Congo Basin
items.standfirst: Yale researchers helped describe Likweli, a rare new Colobus monkey species from the Democratic Republic of Congo. The species was identified using physical traits, genetics, and vocalizations, and appears to have a small range in Lomami National Park and nearby forest. Researchers say the findings highlight both the region’s biodiversity and the need for conservation. [news.yale.edu]
items.content: Yale researchers helped describe Likweli, a rare new Colobus monkey species from the Democratic Republic of Congo. The species was identified using physical traits, genetics, and vocalizations, and appears to have a small range in Lomami National Park and nearby forest. Researchers say the findings highlight both the region’s biodiversity and the need for conservation. [news.yale.edu]

clusters.title: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remain
clusters.neutral_headline: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remain
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, France24

items.title: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remain
items.standfirst: The new bill also allows the adoption of male distant relatives aged over 15 back into the imperial family.
items.content: The new bill also allows the adoption of male distant relatives aged over 15 back into the imperial family.

items.title: Japan changes imperial succession law but keeps ban on female emperor
items.standfirst: Japan's parliament on Friday enacted changes to imperial succession rules, allowing male distant relatives to rejoin the imperial family and women to retain royal status after marrying commoners. The legislation, however, keeps the ban on female emperors despite opinion polls showing broad public support for allowing women to ascend the Chrysanthemum Throne.
items.content: Japan's parliament on Friday enacted changes to imperial succession rules, allowing male distant relatives to rejoin the imperial family and women to retain royal status after marrying commoners. The legislation, however, keeps the ban on female emperors despite opinion polls showing broad public support for allowing women to ascend the Chrysanthemum Throne.

clusters.title: Trump tightens US visa rules, limits stay for foreign students and journalists
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump tightens US visa rules, limits stay for foreign students and journalists
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Trump tightens US visa rules, limits stay for foreign students and journalists
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump's administration on Thursday finalized rules imposing stricter limits on how long foreign students and journalists may stay in the United States, tightening legal immigration. Student visas would be capped at four years, while journalists would be limited to 240-day stays. Chinese journalists would face 90-day limits.
items.content: President Donald Trump's administration on Thursday finalized rules imposing stricter limits on how long foreign students and journalists may stay in the United States, tightening legal immigration. Student visas would be capped at four years, while journalists would be limited to 240-day stays. Chinese journalists would face 90-day limits.

items.title: Trump administration tightens visa rules for foreign students
items.standfirst: Students will need federal permission to stay longer than four years in the US and universities will lose the power to extend visas.
items.content: Students will need federal permission to stay longer than four years in the US and universities will lose the power to extend visas.

items.title: Netflix Feeds Investor Anxiety With Disappointing Forecast
items.standfirst: Netflix Inc. forecast a second consecutive quarter of slowing sales growth, contributing to investor anxiety about the streaming giant’s future. The company projected revenue of $12.9 billion in the current quarter and earnings of 82 cents a share, both a little shy of analysts’ expectations.
items.content: Netflix Inc. forecast a second consecutive quarter of slowing sales growth, contributing to investor anxiety about the streaming giant’s future. clusters.neutral_headline: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
bullet_summary: Reported by Sky News World, Guardian World

items.title: Hundreds feared dead after boats carrying refugees 'capsize' in Bay of Bengal
items.standfirst: It's feared more than 500 people have died after reports that two boats carrying persecuted Rohingya refugees capsized in the Bay of Bengal.
items.content: It's feared more than 500 people have died after reports that two boats carrying persecuted Rohingya refugees capsized in the Bay of Bengal.

items.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
items.standfirst: Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboardThe United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June.The UN’s International Organization for Migration (IOM) and its refugee agency UNHCR voiced alarm in a joint statement at reports “that two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days”. Continue reading...
items.content: Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboardThe United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June.The UN’s International Organization for Migration (IOM) and its refugee agency UNHCR voiced alarm in a joint statement at reports “that two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days”. clusters.title: Jurassic Park star died from pneumonia, agent says
clusters.neutral_headline: Actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, agent confirms
bullet_summary: Jurassic Park and Peaky Blinders star Sam Neill died from pneumonia after beating cancer, his agent said
Reported by Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Jurassic Park star died from pneumonia, agent says
items.standfirst: Jurassic Park and Peaky Blinders star Sam Neill died from pneumonia after beating cancer, his agent said.
items.content: Jurassic Park and Peaky Blinders star Sam Neill died from pneumonia after beating cancer, his agent said.

items.title: Actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, agent confirms
items.standfirst: Neill died in Sydney on Monday, with his family saying his death was 'sudden and unexpected'.
items.content: Neill died in Sydney on Monday, with his family saying his death was 'sudden and unexpected'.
